prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Three things, in sequence:

1. Write ./generation-prompt.md as the generator instruction:

risks_fired.evidence: Three things, in sequence: 1. Write... 2. Run... 3. Run... — numbered multi-step prompt; low-probability plan narration before step 1
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body prose already primes student to look for the per-claim table; no change needed.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Run an eval loop using my fixed judge.

Fixed judge:
- Never edit judges/groundedness-ju

risks_fired.evidence: 300+ word nested loop-architecture prompt (Fixed judge / Starting generation prompt / For each round / When the loop stops) — Claude nearly always opens with plan narration on prompts of this structural complexity before dispatching any subagent
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Start round 1 immediately — no plan narration' or add body callout naming the preamble as expected and telling student to watch for the SHA line as the first proof token.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Run at least 3 rounds. After round 3, keep going until there is no longer significant improvement' — open-ended multi-round orchestration with a complex stopping criterion reliably triggers an extended plan before round 1 begins
risks_fired.fix_hint: Pair with body callout: 'Claude will describe the loop before starting; skip to the SHA or round-1 folder creation to confirm the loop is running.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Claude: can we scale this by adding more generation tracks to try more options faster?
Claude: what is the similarity to model training here? Tell me in 5 snippets, one at a time.

risks_fired.evidence: '5 snippets, one at a time. After each snippet, wait for me to say next.' — structural enforcement is present; residual risk only
risks_fired.fix_hint: Already enforced structurally; no change needed.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Show me module-6/eval-notes.md and ./generation-prompt.md.

Then answer in five bullet

risks_fired.evidence: 'Which failure did the generation prompt still not absorb?' — self-critique without over-flag instruction; mitigated by body-callout carve-out ('Push back where it is too neat' names the failure mode and gives the student the recovery move)
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body-callout carve-out applies; no prompt edit needed.

risks_fired.evidence: 'What was the score trajectory?' — self-report bullet; mitigated because prompt first reads eval-notes.md as the evidence surface
risks_fired.fix_hint: Artifact-evidence carve-out applies; no change needed.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Set up a fixed-judge loop for one of my outputs.

Run the loop end to end:
1. Write a

risks_fired.evidence: 'the main session reads the judgment and rewrites the generation prompt file before the next round' — no explicit overwrite instruction; Claude may pause between rounds to ask before overwriting
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'overwrite the generation prompt file in place' to item 4 of the loop spec.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Run the loop end to end: 1. Write... 2. Dispatch... 3. Dispatch... 4. The main session reads...' — same architectural multi-step shape as Prompt 2 invites plan narration before action
risks_fired.fix_hint: Take-home prompt; optional body note or 'start immediately' addition if students report disorientation.

maintainer_acceptance.note: Prompt 2 preamble-before-action + plan-mode-preamble-bloat (both high) accepted-by-design. Body line 45 instructs student to walk away ("The point is not to watch Claude type"); the loop runs unattended and student returns to the tightened generator artifact, not the narration. Walk-away framing absorbs preamble cost per check_prompts.md rule 17.
